channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Road Lines Faded, 7th June
item.description:
Stop line, pedestrians stud, zigzag faded on both approaches- requires repainting.
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Drugs and drug deals, 7th June
item.description:
I am writing to urgently flag a serious, escalating community safety issue in Broadwater park that requires immediate intervention from both the Council and the local Neighbourhood Policing Team. Every evening, there is blatant, open drug activity and drug dealing taking place directly adjacent to heronsgate primary school park/alleyway. This activity is happening daily, specifically during the early evening hours, and it is deteriorating rapidly. As this is occurring right next to a school, it poses a severe safeguarding risk to local children and youths, as well as a direct threat to the safety and peace of residents in the immediate area. I am contacting the Council to request urgent joint action. Specifically, I ask that you: Coordinate with the Local Policing Team to establish targeted evening hotspot patrols in this specific area. Review Local Infrastructure: Assess whether temporary mobile CCTV, trimmed foliage for better visibility, or improved street lighting can be deployed immediately to deter this activity. Liaise with the School: Ensure the school's leadership team is briefed so they can reinforce safety awareness for students and parents. Our community, and particularly our children, should not have to tolerate open drug markets on their doorstep. I look forward to hearing what immediate steps the Council and your community safety partners will be taking to address this hotspot. Thank you for your prompt attention to this critical safeguarding matter.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Pettman Crescent, Plumstead, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Ongoing fly tipping issue, 7th June
item.description:
With zero suprise it's back again . I get you don't want to investigate. If you don't it's never gonna stop. Maybe if you won't investigate it you could just send around someone to clean this up every week. The food and waste is torn out of the bags every time by foxes and birds.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Drummond Road, Bermondsey, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Fly tipping, 7th June
item.description:
Fly tipping all over this neighborhood on every road and corner

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Harrier Mews, Thamesmead
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Tree on corner needs to be trimmed, 7th June
item.description:
The tree has grown extensively out of bounds for the space, and its branches are now severely blocking both the public footpath and local parking spaces. This is causing a few urgent issues: Pedestrian Obstruction: Passersby, including those with prams or mobility issues, are being forced off the pavement to bypass the branches. Loss of Parking: It is actively obstructing parking bays, making it impossible to park safely without risking damage to vehicles. Visibility & Safety: The overgrowth is limiting visibility for drivers and pedestrians navigating the street. I have attached photos of the current situation to illustrate the extent of the blockage. Could you please arrange for an arboricultural team to inspect and trim this tree as soon as possible to restore full access to the path and parking? Thank you for your prompt attention to this matter. I look forward to your update.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Avocet Mews, Thamesmead
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Long plants : weeds near metal by the fishing lake, 7th June
item.description:
The long grass weeds and bushes near the metal bollards need cutting back please it’s not nice to walk through there when you can’t see what’s in the bushes while a women walking my dog .

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Brook Way, St Paul's Cray, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: "SLOW" road sign on Road worn out, 7th June
item.description:
The SLOW sign painted on road west bound on Hurst Road outside number 120 Hurst Road has worn out and therefore not visible to drivers who often speed down Hurst Road ( between Station Road and Foots Cray Road.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 114, Hurst Road, Albany Park, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Royston Field 2: Park pin noticeboard has gone missing at the Franklin Rd (Sainsbury's) entrance., 7th June
item.description:
Royston Field 2: Park pin noticeboard has gone missing at the Franklin Rd (Sainsbury's) entrance. It was about 1.5m wide x 1m high on a pair of 1.5m uprights - all wooden.

item.title: Dumped clothes airer, 7th June

Please can this clothes airer be collected from next to the phone/internet exchange cabinet on Freelands Road, near to the junction with Widmore Road?

Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Abandoned building, active waste of utilitys, 7th June
item.description:
The outdoor tap has been on for months and the fence is padlocked so it’s a waste of water and utility and we hear what sounds like an air conditioner on its way to no longer working banging every now and again, possible squatters , overgrown uncared for bushes, and could be a useful electric / pay for car park for residents who can’t find a spot or could be flats

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Maismore Street, Peckham, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Fire access driveway blocked, 7th June
item.description:
As always fire and emergency access blocked by multi vehicles and blocking access to 4 garages. Cannot get in or out.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Kyverdale Road, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet
